mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of linear search in the worst case?
mcqs.explanation: Linear search has O(n) worst-case complexity because it may need to check every element in the list.

mcqs.question: Binary search requires the data to be:
mcqs.explanation: Binary search only works on sorted data because it relies on comparing with the middle element to eliminate half the search space.

mcqs.question: Which sorting algorithm repeatedly finds the minimum element and places it in position?
mcqs.explanation: Selection Sort works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted part and placing it at the beginning.

mcqs.question: The best case time complexity of Bubble Sort is:
mcqs.explanation: The best case for Bubble Sort is O(n) when the array is already sorted and the optimized version detects no swaps in a pass.

mcqs.question: Which sorting algorithm is most efficient for nearly sorted data?
mcqs.explanation: Insertion Sort is most efficient for nearly sorted data with O(n) best-case complexity, as few elements need to be moved.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of binary search?
mcqs.explanation: Binary search has O(log n) time complexity because it halves the search space with each comparison.

mcqs.question: A sorting algorithm is said to be stable if:

options.text: It always runs in O(n log n)
It preserves the relative order of equal elements

mcqs.explanation: A stable sorting algorithm maintains the relative order of records with equal keys (values).
